[user]tell me a story about motion.
[Ninereeds]First, Lily sat in the car and watched her father start the engine. She felt the vibration through the seat as the metal parts inside the hood began to move. The turning parts made the wheels spin. Lily could see the rotation begin beneath the car and carry them forward.

The car rolled forward across the gravel road. "If the pistons stop moving, then the wheels will stop turning," her father said. Lily understood that the vibration from the engine shook the whole car as it moved. She kept her hand on the seat to feel the shaking continue.

After that, the road turned bumpy with loose stones. The tires had to grip the rough ground to keep the rotation steady. Lily felt the vibration through the floor and her seat at the same time.

Finally, Lily looked at the spinning wheels and felt the steady vibration through the door panel. She knew that motion came from the engine's parts working together. If the parts kept turning, then the car would keep moving forward.
